items.title: Saline Township Slashes Oracle Data Center Tax Break to Under $20M a Year
items.dek: Trustees approved the 12-year abatement on the project's original $4.8 billion valuation, not the current $43 billion, and added a clawback if the project stalls.

items.title: Power Companies Seek Eminent Domain Authority for Data Center Infrastructure Amid AI Boom
items.dek: Legal battles are emerging over whether utilities can seize private land solely to support data center expansion, with Michigan's Supreme Court among those that have restricted such takings for private economic gain.

items.title: Michigan city keeps data center moratorium in place after denial
items.dek: Local officials rejected a request to lift the pause, keeping new proposals on hold. Residents avoid immediate spikes in power and water use from hyperscale builds. The decision preserves time for better planning on grid strain.
items.source_title: City officials in Michigan denied request to lift data center moratorium - Data Center Dynamics

items.title: Wortz blocks eminent domain for data centers and renewables
items.dek: The state representative says data centers and green energy projects cannot seize private land in Michigan. The stance protects property owners in rural areas where large facilities are proposed. It signals growing legislative pushback on rapid buildouts.
items.source_title: Rep. Wortz: Data Centers and Green Energy Don’t Have Eminent Domain - Michigan House Republicans

items.title: GOP governor hopeful backs statewide data center freeze
items.dek: A candidate for Michigan governor is calling for a pause on new data centers, citing concerns over power demand and local impacts. The position could influence voter debates in the upcoming primary. Michigan residents face rising utility costs tied to these projects.
items.source_title: Governor candidate supports moratorium for future data centers - The Daily Reporter

items.title: Michigan Farmers and Lawmakers Rally Against Saline Data Center Traffic and Land Use
items.dek: State Sen. Ruth Johnson joined protesters outside the Capitol as the Saline Barn project rises on township farmland, with residents posting 'No Data Center Traffic' signs along Michigan Avenue.
items.judge_note: New development (state senator joining protest, physical signs posted) but incremental follow-up to already-covered Saline opposition rather than a concrete dec
